aWSLambda.serviceDescription: AWS Lambda lets you run code without provisioning or managing servers. You pay only for the compute time you consume - there is no charge when your code is not running.

templates.title: Lambda Function -  Include Free Tier
templates.description: Lambda counts a request each time it starts executing in response to an event notification or invoke call, including test invokes from the console. You are charged for the total number of requests across all your functions. The price depends on the amount of memory you allocate to  your function. The Lambda free tier includes 1M free requests per month and 400,000 GB-seconds of compute time per month.

cards.title: Service settings
cards.helpText: With AWS Lambda, you pay only for what you use. You are charged based on the number of requests for your functions and the duration, the time it takes for your code to execute.

components.label: Duration of each request (in ms)
components.description: Duration is calculated from the time your code begins executing until it returns or otherwise terminates.

components.label: Amount of memory allocated
components.description: Enter the amount between 128 MB and 10 GB

components.label: Amount of ephemeral storage allocated
components.description: Enter the amount between 512 MB and 10,240 MB. The first 512 MB are at no additional charge, you only pay for any additional storage that you configure for the function.

components.label: price for lambda duration single tier
info alert
components.content: The Lambda free tier includes 1M free requests per month and 400,000 GB-seconds of compute time per month.

operands.variableLabel: price for lambda duration single tier

components.outputUnitLabel: GB billable ephemeral storage per function

operands.variableLabel: GB billable ephemeral storage per function

cards.title: Provisioned Concurrency
cards.helpText: You can enable Provisioned Concurrency for your Lambda functions for greater control over the performance of your serverless applications. When enabled, Provisioned Concurrency keeps functions initialized and hyper-ready to respond in double-digit milliseconds. You pay for the amount of concurrency that you configure and for the period of time that you configure it.

components.label: Concurrency
components.description: Enter the concurrency configured for the function

components.label: Time for which Provisioned Concurrency is enabled
components.description: Enter the time period when Provisioned Concurrency is enabled for your function over the whole month

components.label: Number of requests for Provisioned Concurrency
components.description: Enter the number of requests that you will make when the function is configured with Provisioned Concurrency

components.label: Duration of each provisioned request (in ms)
components.description: Duration is calculated from the time your code begins executing until it returns or otherwise terminates.

components.label: Amount of memory allocated
components.description: Enter the amount between 128 MB and 10 GB

operands.variableLabel: Time for which Provisioned Concurrency is enabled
Number of requests for Provisioned Concurrency
Duration of each provisioned request (in ms)
Number of requests for Provisioned Concurrency

cards.title: SnapStart
cards.helpText: SnapStart can improve startup performance from several seconds to as low as sub-second for latency sensitive applications. SnapStart works by snapshotting your function's initialized memory and disk state, and caching this snapshot for low-latency access. You are charged for caching a snapshot over the period that your function version is active and each time Lambda resumes an execution environment by restoring your snapshot.

components.label: Time for which SnapStart is enabled
components.description: Enter the time period when SnapStart is enabled for your function over the whole month

components.helpText: Enter the monthly number of requests that are cold starts, using the CloudWatch Insights query listed on this page with production traffic volume.
components.label: Number of cold-starts (i.e. SnapStart restores)
components.description: Enter the number of function requests that are cold-starts when SnapStart is enabled

components.label: Amount of memory allocated
components.description: Enter the amount between 128 MB and 10 GB

operands.variableLabel: Time for which SnapStart is enabled

cards.title: Lambda@Edge
cards.helpText: There is no free tier for Lambda@Edge at this time. You are charged for the total number of requests across all your functions. Lambda@Edge counts a request each time it starts executing in response to a CloudFront event globally.

components.text: There is no free tier for Lambda@Edge at this time. Lambda@Edge pricing does not vary by region. You are charged for the total number of requests across all your functions executed in all regions.

components.label: Duration of each request (in ms)
components.description: Duration is calculated from the time your code begins executing until it returns or otherwise terminates.

components.label: Amount of memory allocated
components.description: Enter the amount between 128 MB and 10 GB

cards.title: Lambda HTTP Response Streaming
cards.helpText: AWS Lambda functions can return an HTTP response stream when invoked via the InvokeWithResponseStream API or through a function URL using the ResponseStream invoke mode. When using HTTP response streaming, you are charged for each GB written to the response stream by your function. You can stream the first 6MB per request at no cost.

components.text: There is no additional charge on buffered response.

operands.constantUnitLabel: MB free of a given response payload

components.outputUnitLabel: MB of processed bytes billable per invoke

operands.variableLabel: MB of processed bytes billable per invoke

templates.title: Lambda Function -  Without Free Tier

You are charged for the total number of requests across all your functions. The price depends on the amount of memory you allocate to  your function. The Lambda free tier discounts are excluded
